OTX2 case study — Fig 3g¶

Distal OTX2 enrichment heatmap across 4C embryos, primed hESCs and derived ectoderm-like cells (dEC) (Tsankov 2015, Gifford 2013). Peaks are partitioned into five cell-type-specific clusters. Paper observes ~85% of 4C distal peaks are 4C-specific.

Paper: Wang et al. Nat. Genet. 57, 2772-2784 (2025) 10.1038/s41588-025-02350-8

Upstream processing (documented, not executed)¶

The cells in this section are wrapped in %%script false --no-raise-error so they do not run; they exist to document the full pipeline from raw FASTQ / GEO accession down to the cached inputs that the runnable cells below consume. Run them once from a shell if you are rebuilding /scratch/users/steorra/data/otx2/ from scratch.

Reference (hg19 + GENCODE)¶

In [ ]:
%%script false --no-raise-error
# ==========================================================
# hg19 reference + GENCODE v41lift37 annotation + bowtie2 index
# ==========================================================
cd /scratch/users/steorra/data/hg19
wget -c https://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/hg19/bigZips/hg19.fa.gz
gunzip hg19.fa.gz && samtools faidx hg19.fa
cut -f1,2 hg19.fa.fai > hg19.chrom.sizes

wget -c https://ftp.ebi.ac.uk/pub/databases/gencode/Gencode_human/release_41/GRCh37_mapping/gencode.v41lift37.annotation.gtf.gz

bowtie2-build --threads 8 hg19.fa bt2_idx/hg19

OMIX006476 processed 4C / ESC bigwigs + peaks¶

In [ ]:
%%script false --no-raise-error
# ==========================================================
# OMIX006476 (Wang 2025) processed bigwigs, peaks, metadata
# ==========================================================
# Full archive for the paper's own CUT&RUN / ATAC / ChIP datasets.
wget -c https://ngdc.cncb.ac.cn/omix/files/OMIX006476/OMIX006476-02.zip \
    -O /scratch/users/steorra/data/otx2/OMIX006476-02.zip
unzip -d /scratch/users/steorra/data/otx2/OMIX006476 \
      /scratch/users/steorra/data/otx2/OMIX006476-02.zip

Supplementary table S7 (4C peaks)¶

In [ ]:
%%script false --no-raise-error
# ==========================================================
# Nature Genetics supplementary tables S1-S7
# ==========================================================
# Downloaded from the article HTML (Springer). Save as:
#   /scratch/users/steorra/data/otx2/supp/supp_tables.xlsx
# The sheet names used throughout these notebooks:
#   Table S3_stage-activated genes   — per-stage activated gene lists
#   Table S4_OTX2 KD_RNA count       — raw count matrix for RNA-KD
#   Table S6_DEG                     — DESeq2 DEG blocks (D2-KD, D3-KD, ...)
#   Table S7_4C OTX2_CUTRUN_peaks    — 4C OTX2 peak list (n=45,521)
echo "See paper Supplementary Information on the Nature article page."

CUT&RUN FASTQ → bigwig (paper's 4C CUT&RUN)¶

In [ ]:
%%script false --no-raise-error
# ==========================================================
# CUT&RUN FASTQ -> deduplicated BAM -> RPKM-normalised bigwig
# ==========================================================
# Reference: Wang et al. 2025 Methods ("CUT&RUN assay" section) +
# filename convention in OMIX006476 (`..._pmu_100bp_rpkm_bamCoverage.bw`).
#
# Raw FASTQs are hosted behind controlled access at GSA-Human HRA006621
# (paper Data Availability). For each replicate:
SAMPLE=h_OTX2_day2_CUTRUN_rep1         # one of rep1 / rep2 / KD etc.
FQDIR=/scratch/users/steorra/data/otx2/HRA006621/fastq
BAMDIR=/scratch/users/steorra/data/otx2/OMIX006476/bam
BWDIR=/scratch/users/steorra/data/otx2/OMIX006476
IDX=/scratch/users/steorra/data/hg19/bt2_idx/hg19
mkdir -p $BAMDIR $BWDIR

# 1. Adapter / quality trim
trim_galore --paired --gzip -o $BAMDIR/trim \
    $FQDIR/${SAMPLE}_R1.fastq.gz $FQDIR/${SAMPLE}_R2.fastq.gz

# 2. Bowtie2 alignment (paired, CUT&RUN-optimised)
bowtie2 --end-to-end --very-sensitive --no-mixed --no-discordant \
        -I 10 -X 700 -p 8 -x $IDX \
        -1 $BAMDIR/trim/${SAMPLE}_R1_val_1.fq.gz \
        -2 $BAMDIR/trim/${SAMPLE}_R2_val_2.fq.gz |
    samtools view -bS -q 30 -f 2 - |
    samtools sort -@ 8 -o $BAMDIR/${SAMPLE}.raw.bam

# 3. Picard MarkDuplicates -> "pmu" (picard mark unique) BAM
picard MarkDuplicates \
    I=$BAMDIR/${SAMPLE}.raw.bam \
    O=$BAMDIR/${SAMPLE}.pmu.bam \
    M=$BAMDIR/${SAMPLE}.pmu.metrics REMOVE_DUPLICATES=true
samtools index $BAMDIR/${SAMPLE}.pmu.bam

# 4. Merge biological replicates (rep1 + rep2 -> rep12)
samtools merge -@ 8 -o $BAMDIR/h_OTX2_day2_CUTRUN_rep12.pmu.bam \
    $BAMDIR/h_OTX2_day2_CUTRUN_rep1.pmu.bam \
    $BAMDIR/h_OTX2_day2_CUTRUN_rep2.pmu.bam
samtools index $BAMDIR/h_OTX2_day2_CUTRUN_rep12.pmu.bam

# 5. RPKM bigwig (matches published file name)
bamCoverage -b $BAMDIR/h_OTX2_day2_CUTRUN_rep12.pmu.bam \
    -o $BWDIR/h_OTX2_day2_CUTRUN_rep12_pmu_100bp_rpkm_bamCoverage.bw \
    --binSize 100 --normalizeUsing RPKM -p 8

MACS2 peak calling (Supp Table S7)¶

In [ ]:
%%script false --no-raise-error
# ==========================================================
# MACS2 peak calling on CUT&RUN BAMs
# (Supp Table S7 peaks + h_OTX2_day2_CUTRUN_rep12_peaks.bed)
# ==========================================================
BAMDIR=/scratch/users/steorra/data/otx2/OMIX006476/bam
PEAKDIR=/scratch/users/steorra/data/otx2/OMIX006476/peaks
mkdir -p $PEAKDIR

macs2 callpeak \
    -t $BAMDIR/h_OTX2_day2_CUTRUN_rep12.pmu.bam \
    -f BAMPE -g hs --nomodel --call-summits \
    --keep-dup all -q 0.01 \
    -n h_OTX2_day2_CUTRUN_rep12 --outdir $PEAKDIR
# -> $PEAKDIR/h_OTX2_day2_CUTRUN_rep12_peaks.narrowPeak  (distributed as _peaks.bed)
# Supp Table S7 is this peak set curated by the paper authors.

Gifford 2013 dEC bigwig + peaks¶

In [ ]:
%%script false --no-raise-error
# ==========================================================
# Gifford et al. 2013 Cell (GSE61475) — derived ectoderm (dEC) OTX2 ChIP-seq
# ==========================================================
# Paper ref 62. Already-processed bigwig + peak tables.
cd /scratch/users/steorra/data/otx2/GSE61475
for acc in GSM1505707_Otx2_022813_ecto GSM1505710_Otx2_062613_ecto ; do
  GSM=${acc%%_*}
  base=ftp://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/samples/${GSM:0:7}nnn/${GSM}/suppl/${acc}
  wget -c "${base}.bw"
  wget -c "${base}.bed.peak.txt.gz"
done
# Gifford chromosome column is bare numeric ("1", not "chr1"); handled in Python.

1 · Setup¶

In [2]:
import os, pathlib, warnings
os.environ['PATH'] = '/scratch/users/steorra/env/omicdev/bin:' + os.environ.get('PATH', '')
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore', category=UserWarning)

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

import epione as epi
epi.pl.plot_set()

DATA         = pathlib.Path('/scratch/users/steorra/data/otx2')
OMIX         = DATA / 'OMIX006476'
ZOU          = DATA / 'GSE197265'
XIA          = DATA / 'GSE124718'
GIFFORD      = DATA / 'GSE61475'
SUPP_TABLES  = DATA / 'supp/supp_tables.xlsx'
GENCODE_GTF  = pathlib.Path('/scratch/users/steorra/data/hg19/gencode.v41lift37.annotation.gtf.gz')

2 · Load distal peak sets for the three conditions¶

4C from Supp Table S7, ESC from OMIX H9 ChIP peak BED, dEC from Gifford peak-txt (which uses bare numeric chrom names). Paper Methods: distal = ≥ 2.5 kb from nearest TSS.

In [3]:
s7 = pd.read_excel(SUPP_TABLES, sheet_name='Table S7_4C OTX2_CUTRUN_peaks',
                     header=None, names=['chrom','start','end','name','score'])
peaks_4C = s7.dropna(subset=['chrom','start','end'])[['chrom','start','end']].astype({'start':int,'end':int})
peaks_ESC = pd.read_csv(OMIX / 'H9_OTX2_ChIP_21Mcells_peaks.bed',
                          sep='\t', header=None, usecols=[0,1,2], names=['chrom','start','end'])
dec_dfs = []
for f in [GIFFORD/'GSM1505707_Otx2_022813_ecto.bed.peak.txt.gz',
            GIFFORD/'GSM1505710_Otx2_062613_ecto.bed.peak.txt.gz']:
    d = pd.read_csv(f, sep='\t', header=None, usecols=[0,1,2],
                     names=['chrom','start','end'], dtype={'chrom':str})
    d['chrom'] = 'chr' + d['chrom']
    dec_dfs.append(d)
peaks_dEC = pd.concat(dec_dfs, ignore_index=True)
print(f'4C={len(peaks_4C)}  ESC={len(peaks_ESC)}  dEC={len(peaks_dEC)}')

4C=45521  ESC=70126  dEC=30714
In [4]:
gtf = epi.utils.get_gene_annotation(str(GENCODE_GTF))
gtf['tss'] = np.where(gtf['strand'] == '+', gtf['start'], gtf['end'])
tss_tbl = gtf[['chrom','tss']]

def _distal(peaks):
    peaks = peaks.copy()
    peaks['center'] = (peaks['start']+peaks['end'])//2
    dists = epi.utils.distance_to_nearest_peak(
        peaks.rename(columns={'center':'tss'}), tss_tbl,
        feature_col='tss', peak_center='tss', unit='bp')
    # distance_to_nearest_peak drops rows without same-chrom peaks; keep
    # original rows instead by computing directly here.
    mask = np.ones(len(peaks), dtype=bool)
    tss_by_c = {c: np.sort(g['tss'].values) for c, g in tss_tbl.groupby('chrom')}
    for i, (c, ctr) in enumerate(zip(peaks['chrom'].values, peaks['center'].values)):
        arr = tss_by_c.get(c)
        if arr is None: continue
        j = np.searchsorted(arr, ctr)
        d_bp = min([abs(int(arr[k])-int(ctr)) for k in (j-1,j) if 0<=k<len(arr)], default=1e9)
        if d_bp < 2500: mask[i] = False
    return peaks[mask].reset_index(drop=True)

distal_4C  = _distal(peaks_4C)
distal_ESC = _distal(peaks_ESC)
distal_dEC = _distal(peaks_dEC)
print(f'distal: 4C={len(distal_4C)} ESC={len(distal_ESC)} dEC={len(distal_dEC)}')

distal: 4C=41286 ESC=61892 dEC=22623

3 · Assign each peak to a cell-type cluster¶

For every 4C peak, overlap-check against ESC and dEC peak intervals; same for ESC-not-in-4C and dEC-not-in-either. Produces five groups.

In [5]:
def _idx(df):
    out = {}
    for c, g in df.groupby('chrom'):
        s = g['start'].values; e = g['end'].values
        o = np.argsort(s); out[c] = (s[o], e[o])
    return out

def _ov(df, other):
    out = np.zeros(len(df), dtype=bool)
    for i, (c, s, e) in enumerate(df[['chrom','start','end']].itertuples(index=False)):
        if c not in other: continue
        st, en = other[c]
        k = np.searchsorted(st, e); lo = max(0, k-1)
        if np.any((st[lo:k] < e) & (en[lo:k] > s)): out[i] = True
    return out

idx_4C, idx_ESC, idx_dEC = _idx(distal_4C), _idx(distal_ESC), _idx(distal_dEC)
rows_4C = distal_4C.copy()
in_E = _ov(distal_4C, idx_ESC); in_D = _ov(distal_4C, idx_dEC)
rows_4C['cluster'] = np.where(in_E & in_D, '4C/ESC/dEC shared',
                       np.where(in_E, '4C/ESC shared',
                       np.where(in_D, '4C/dEC shared', '4C-specific')))
rows_ESC = distal_ESC[~_ov(distal_ESC, idx_4C)].copy()
rows_ESC['cluster'] = np.where(_ov(rows_ESC, idx_dEC), 'ESC/dEC shared', 'ESC-specific')
rows_dEC = distal_dEC[~_ov(distal_dEC, idx_4C) & ~_ov(distal_dEC, idx_ESC)].copy()
rows_dEC['cluster'] = 'dEC-specific'
all_rows = pd.concat([rows_4C, rows_ESC, rows_dEC], ignore_index=True)
print(all_rows['cluster'].value_counts())

cluster
ESC-specific         43041
4C-specific          28211
dEC-specific         10081
4C/ESC shared         9724
ESC/dEC shared        5298
4C/ESC/dEC shared     2718
4C/dEC shared          633
Name: count, dtype: int64

4 · Heatmap via bigwig.plot_matrix_multi¶

Multi-condition × multi-cluster heatmap with left-side coloured cluster bar. max_per_cluster caps each cluster's rows — pulls 5 × 3 k peaks instead of 100 k and finishes in ~30 s. Per-column 98th-percentile vmax handles the different absolute scales between 4C, ESC and dEC bigwigs.

In [6]:
bw = epi.bulk.bigwig({
    '4C':  str(OMIX    / 'h_OTX2_day2_CUTRUN_rep12_pmu_100bp_rpkm_bamCoverage.bw'),
    'ESC': str(OMIX    / 'H9_OTX2_ChIP_21Mcells_100bp_rpkm_bamCoverage.bw'),
    'dEC': str(GIFFORD / 'GSM1505707_Otx2_022813_ecto.bw'),
})
bw.read()

fig, axes = bw.plot_matrix_multi(
    bw_names=['4C', 'ESC', 'dEC'],
    regions=all_rows,
    cluster_col='cluster',
    cluster_order=['4C-specific','ESC-specific','dEC-specific',
                    '4C/ESC shared','4C/ESC/dEC shared'],
    cluster_colors={'4C-specific':'#2A9D4C','ESC-specific':'#3A6EB3',
                     'dEC-specific':'#B03A3A','4C/ESC shared':'#7A4FA8',
                     '4C/ESC/dEC shared':'#C78A3A'},
    max_per_cluster=3000,
    upstream=3000, downstream=3000, nbins=120,
    figsize=(4.2, 7.5), title='Distal OTX2 binding',
)
plt.show()
